Label: Rationale
Comment: A summary explaining why this indicator matters—its relevance to population health, what it helps to understand, and how it complements other indicators.
Source: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13
Creator: Rob Brennan, ADAPT UCD

Label: Disaggregations
Comment: A list of disaggregation conditions that should be available e.g. male and female
Source: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13
Creator: Rob Brennan, ADAPT UCD

Label: Numerator Data Element
Comment: The description of one variable or value that is used in the numerator e.g. number of deaths. Multiple data elements may be present as a list.
Source: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13
Creator: Rob Brennan, ADAPT UCD

Label: Denominator Data Element
Comment: The description of one variable or value that is used in the denominator (if this indicator has one) e.g. population size. Multiple data elements may be present as a list.
Source: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13
Creator: Rob Brennan, ADAPT UCD

Label: Methodology
Comment: Summary of methods used to calculate the indicator (e.,g statistical technique, smoothing, standardisation, modelling).
Source: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13
Creator: Rob Brennan, ADAPT UCD

Label: High-Low Guidance
Comment: Guidance on how to understand high or low values—whether they suggest good or poor outcomes.
Source: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13
Creator: Rob Brennan, ADAPT UCD

Label: Measurement Limitations
Comment: Notes on limitations or uncertainty introduced by how the data are collected or calculated.
Source: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13
Creator: Rob Brennan, ADAPT UCD

Label: Validity Guidance
Comment: Description of how the indicator might be influenced by other variables or biases, affecting validity.
Source: OAHP Data Catalogue Schema Proposal v05.13
Creator: Rob Brennan, ADAPT UCD

Label: Status
Comment: An internal prioritisation process status for the dataset or indicator
Creator: Rob Brennan, ADAPT UCD
